name: Benkei Full
description: Saitō Musashibō Benkei, popularly known as Benkei, was a warrior monk (sōhei) who lived in Japan in the latter years of the Heian period (794–1185). He is a popular figure in Japanese folklore, known for his immense strength, unwavering loyalty, and legendary death. Benkei is often depicted as a towering figure, clad in monk's robes and wielding a variety of weapons, most notably a naginata. He served Minamoto no Yoshitsune, a prominent general, and is renowned for his bravery and dedication to his lord. Benkei's origins are shrouded in legend, with some accounts claiming he was the son of a temple deity. He is said to have roamed the streets of Kyoto, challenging warriors to duels and collecting their swords. He amassed a collection of 999 swords before finally being defeated by Yoshitsune, whom he then swore allegiance to. Benkei fought alongside Yoshitsune in the Genpei War and remained loyal to him even after Yoshitsune fell out of favor with his brother, Minamoto no Yoritomo. Benkei's most famous moment came at the Battle of Koromogawa, where he stood guard over Yoshitsune's escape. He is said to have died standing upright, riddled with arrows, a testament to his indomitable spirit and unwavering loyalty. Benkei represents the ideals of Bushido: loyalty, courage, and self-sacrifice. His story continues to inspire and captivate audiences in Japan and beyond.
subtitle: Loyal Warrior Monk of Japan
achievements: Serving Minamoto no Yoshitsune with unwavering loyalty
Fighting valiantly in the Genpei War
Dying a legendary death at Koromogawa
weapons: Various swords collected from defeated warriors

keyMyths.title: The Collection of 999 Swords
keyMyths.description: Benkei roamed Kyoto, challenging warriors and collecting their swords. His goal was to obtain 1000 swords, symbolizing his strength. He was eventually defeated by Yoshitsune, preventing him from reaching his goal.

keyMyths.title: Benkei's Loyalty to Yoshitsune
keyMyths.description: Despite Yoshitsune's fall from grace, Benkei remained steadfastly loyal. He protected Yoshitsune at all costs, even sacrificing his own life. This loyalty is a core element of Benkei's legend.

keyMyths.title: The Standing Death at Koromogawa
keyMyths.description: Benkei died standing upright, riddled with arrows, while guarding Yoshitsune's escape. This iconic image symbolizes his unwavering dedication and indomitable spirit. It is a testament to his legendary status.
